data.name: AL0001AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Prohibit reassignment of primary constructor parameters

data.name: AL0001AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Primary constructor parameter '{0}' should not be reassigned

data.name: AL0001AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Primary constructor parameters should not be reassigned as this can lead to confusion and bugs.

data.name: AL0002AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Don't repeat negated patterns

data.name: AL0002AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'not' only once or not at all

data.name: AL0002AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The negated pattern should not be repeated multiple times.

data.name: AL0003AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Don't divide by constant zero

data.name: AL0003AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Division by constant zero will throw DivideByZeroException

data.name: AL0003AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Integers and Decimal should never be divided by the constant 0 as this causes a DivideByZeroException at runtime.

data.name: AL0004AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use pattern matching when comparing Span with constants

data.name: AL0004AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use pattern matching instead of equality operators for Span comparison

data.name: AL0004AnalyzerDescription
data.value: When comparing Span and a constant, use pattern matching instead of equality operators, unless you want to compare addresses.

data.name: AL0005AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use SequenceEqual when comparing Span with non-constants

data.name: AL0005AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use SequenceEqual instead of equality operators for Span comparison

data.name: AL0005AnalyzerDescription
data.value: When comparing Span and a non-constant, use SequenceEqual instead of equality operators, unless you want to compare addresses.

data.name: AL0006AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Field name conflicts with primary constructor parameter

data.name: AL0006AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Field '{0}' conflicts with a primary constructor parameter

data.name: AL0006AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Field names should not conflict with primary constructor parameters to avoid shadowing and confusion.

data.name: AL0007AnalyzerTitle
data.value: GetSchema should be explicitly implemented

data.name: AL0007AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: IXmlSerializable.GetSchema should be explicitly implemented

data.name: AL0007AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Implement IXmlSerializable.GetSchema explicitly to avoid exposing it as part of the public API.

data.name: AL0008AnalyzerTitle
data.value: GetSchema must return null and not be abstract

data.name: AL0008AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: IXmlSerializable.GetSchema should return null and not be abstract

data.name: AL0008AnalyzerDescription
data.value: GetSchema should return null according to IXmlSerializable guidance and should not be abstract.

data.name: AL0009AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Don't call IXmlSerializable.GetSchema

data.name: AL0009AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Don't call IXmlSerializable.GetSchema

data.name: AL0009AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Calling GetSchema is not supported; it should always return null and isn't intended to be invoked.

data.name: AL0010AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Type should be partial

data.name: AL0010AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: The type '{0}' should be partial for source generator support

data.name: AL0010AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Types that are targets for source generators should be marked as partial.

data.name: AL0011AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid lock keyword on non-Lock types

data.name: AL0011AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'Lock _lock = new()' with 'lock(_lock)' instead of 'lock(object)'. The Lock class provides better performance in .NET 9+.

data.name: AL0011AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The lock keyword on object/non-Lock types should be replaced with the Lock class from System.Threading.

data.name: AL0012AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Deprecated semantic convention attribute

data.name: AL0012AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' is deprecated since schema v{1}. Use '{2}' instead.

data.name: AL0012AnalyzerDescription
data.value: This attribute name has been deprecated in the OpenTelemetry semantic conventions.

data.name: AL0013AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Missing telemetry schema URL

data.name: AL0013AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: OpenTelemetry resource configuration should include 'telemetry.schema_url' attribute

data.name: AL0013AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Setting the schema URL allows collectors and backends to understand which version of semantic conventions your telemetry uses.

data.name: AL0017AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Hardcoded package version detected

data.name: AL0017AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Package '{0}' uses hardcoded version '{1}'. Use $({2}) from Version.props instead.

data.name: AL0017AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Package versions should use MSBuild variables from Version.props for centralized version management. This ensures consistency across all projects and simplifies version updates.

data.name: AL0020AnalyzerTitle
data.value: IFormCollection requires explicit attribute

data.name: AL0020AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Parameter '{0}' on '{1}' uses IFormCollection but lacks [FromForm]. IFormCollection does not auto-bind; add [FromForm] explicitly.

data.name: AL0020AnalyzerDescription
data.value: IFormCollection parameters must have an explicit [FromForm] attribute. Unlike IFormFile which auto-binds, IFormCollection requires explicit attribution for clarity and safety.

data.name: AL0021AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Multiple structured form sources

data.name: AL0021AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Endpoint '{0}' has multiple [FromForm] parameters. Only one structured form body ([FromForm] DTO or [FromForm] IFormCollection) is allowed.

data.name: AL0021AnalyzerDescription
data.value: An endpoint can only have one structured form source. Multiple [FromForm] DTOs or IFormCollections create ambiguity about which parameter receives the form data.

data.name: AL0022AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Mixed form collection and DTO

data.name: AL0022AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Endpoint '{0}' mixes [FromForm] IFormCollection with [FromForm] DTO. Use either raw IFormCollection or structured DTO binding, not both.

data.name: AL0022AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Mixing IFormCollection with a [FromForm] DTO is not supported. Choose one approach: use IFormCollection for dynamic form access or a typed DTO for structured binding.

data.name: AL0023AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Unsupported form type

data.name: AL0023AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Parameter '{0}' on '{1}' cannot be form-bound: {2}

data.name: AL0023AnalyzerDescription
data.value: [FromForm] can only bind primitives, primitive collections, or DTOs with a parameterless constructor or primary constructor with all-primitive parameters.

data.name: AL0024AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Form and body conflict

data.name: AL0024AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Endpoint '{0}' has both [FromForm] and [FromBody] parameters. An endpoint can read from form data OR JSON body, not both.

data.name: AL0024AnalyzerDescription
data.value: An HTTP request body can only be consumed once. Mixing [FromForm] and [FromBody] parameters in the same endpoint is not supported.

data.name: AL0025AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Anonymous function can be made static

data.name: AL0025AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Anonymous function can be made 'static' since it does not capture any variables

data.name: AL0025AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Lambda expressions and anonymous methods that don't capture variables should be marked 'static' to avoid unnecessary allocations and improve performance.

data.name: AL0026AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid DateTime/DateTimeOffset time accessors

data.name: AL0026AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}.{1}' should be replaced with 'TimeProvider.System.{2}()' for better testability

data.name: AL0026AnalyzerDescription
data.value: DateTime.Now/UtcNow and DateTimeOffset.Now/UtcNow make code difficult to test. Use TimeProvider.System.GetLocalNow() or GetUtcNow() instead, which can be mocked in tests.

data.name: AL0027AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid legacy JSON library

data.name: AL0027AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' is from a legacy JSON library. Use System.Text.Json instead.

data.name: AL0027AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The legacy JSON library should be replaced with System.Text.Json for better performance and native .NET support.

data.name: AL0018AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Version.props not imported

data.name: AL0018AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Directory.Build.props should import Version.props for centralized version management

data.name: AL0018AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Version.props should be imported in Directory.Build.props to enable centralized package version management using $(VariableName) syntax.

data.name: AL0019AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Undefined version variable

data.name: AL0019AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Package '{0}' uses undefined version variable '$({1})'. Define '{1}' in Version.props.

data.name: AL0019AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Version variables used in Directory.Packages.props must be defined in Version.props. Missing definitions cause silent build failures or empty version strings.

data.name: AL0028AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use IsEqualTo extension

data.name: AL0028AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of {1}

data.name: AL0028AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use IsEqualTo extension instead of SymbolEqualityComparer.Default.Equals for cleaner symbol comparison.

data.name: AL0029AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use HasAttribute extension

data.name: AL0029AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of {1}

data.name: AL0029AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use HasAttribute extension instead of GetAttributes() LINQ queries or foreach loops for cleaner attribute checking.

data.name: AL0030AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use type hierarchy extension

data.name: AL0030AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of {1}

data.name: AL0030AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use Implements or InheritsFrom extensions instead of manual AllInterfaces/BaseType iteration loops.

data.name: AL0031AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use operation extension

data.name: AL0031AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of {1}

data.name: AL0031AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use IsMethodNamed or TryGetConstantValue extensions instead of verbose property access patterns.

data.name: AL0032AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use OrEmpty extension

data.name: AL0032AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of {1}

data.name: AL0032AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use OrEmpty() extension instead of null-coalescing with Array.Empty, Enumerable.Empty, or collection expressions.

data.name: AL0033AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use ToImmutableArrayOrEmpty extension

data.name: AL0033AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of {1}

data.name: AL0033AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use ToImmutableArrayOrEmpty() extension instead of null-conditional ToImmutableArray() with ImmutableArray.Empty fallback.

data.name: AL0034AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use WhereNotNull extension

data.name: AL0034AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of {1}

data.name: AL0034AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use WhereNotNull() extension instead of Where(x => x != null) or Where(x => x is not null).

data.name: AL0035AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use symbol display string extension

data.name: AL0035AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of {1}

data.name: AL0035AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use GetFullyQualifiedName() or GetMetadataName() extensions instead of ToDisplayString with SymbolDisplayFormat constants.

data.name: AL0036AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use null-guard helper

data.name: AL0036AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of null-coalescing throw

data.name: AL0036AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use null-guard helpers (Throw.IfNull or Guard.NotNull) instead of verbose null-coalescing throw new ArgumentNullException patterns.

data.name: AL0037AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use TryParse extension

data.name: AL0037AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of verbose TryParse pattern

data.name: AL0037AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use TryParse extension methods (TryParseInt32, TryParseGuid, etc.) instead of verbose Type.TryParse ternary patterns.

data.name: AL0039AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use StringComparison extension

data.name: AL0039AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' for clearer intent

data.name: AL0039AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use StringComparison extensions (EqualsOrdinal, ContainsIgnoreCase, etc.) instead of methods with StringComparison enum parameter.

data.name: AL0040AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use attribute argument extraction extension

data.name: AL0040AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of direct ConstructorArguments/NamedArguments access

data.name: AL0040AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use GetConstructorArgument or GetNamedArgument extensions instead of direct array access on AttributeData.

data.name: AL0041AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Method with [AotTest] or [TrimTest] must return int

data.name: AL0041AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Method '{0}' has [{1}] attribute but returns '{2}' instead of 'int'

data.name: AL0041AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Methods decorated with [AotTest] or [TrimTest] attributes must return int as their exit code. The test harness expects an integer return value to determine success or failure.

data.name: AL0042AnalyzerTitle
data.value: [AotTest]/[TrimTest] method should return 100 on success

data.name: AL0042AnalyzerDescription
data.value: By convention, AOT/Trim test methods should return 100 to indicate success. Other values are reserved for failure conditions.

data.name: AL0043AnalyzerTitle
data.value: [TrimSafe] code must not call methods with [RequiresUnreferencedCode]

data.name: AL0043AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Method '{0}' is marked [TrimSafe] but calls '{1}' which has [RequiresUnreferencedCode]

data.name: AL0043AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Code marked with [TrimSafe] guarantees it will work correctly in trimmed applications. Calling methods with [RequiresUnreferencedCode] violates this guarantee because those methods may fail at runtime when types are trimmed.

data.name: AL0044AnalyzerTitle
data.value: [AotSafe] code must not call methods with [RequiresDynamicCode]

data.name: AL0044AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Method '{0}' is marked [AotSafe] but calls '{1}' which has [RequiresDynamicCode]

data.name: AL0044AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Code marked with [AotSafe] guarantees it will work correctly in AOT-compiled applications. Calling methods with [RequiresDynamicCode] violates this guarantee because those methods rely on runtime code generation which is not available in AOT scenarios.

data.name: AL0045AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use null-or-empty guard helper

data.name: AL0045AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'Guard.NotNullOrEmpty({0})' instead of if/throw pattern

data.name: AL0045AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use Guard.NotNullOrEmpty() instead of verbose if (string.IsNullOrEmpty(...)) throw new ArgumentNullException/ArgumentException patterns.

data.name: AL0046AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use null-or-whitespace guard helper

data.name: AL0046AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'Guard.NotNullOrWhiteSpace({0})' instead of if/throw pattern

data.name: AL0046AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use Guard.NotNullOrWhiteSpace() instead of verbose if (string.IsNullOrWhiteSpace(...)) throw new ArgumentNullException/ArgumentException patterns.

data.name: AL0047AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use zero-guard helper

data.name: AL0047AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'Guard.NotZero({0})' instead of if/throw pattern

data.name: AL0047AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use Guard.NotZero() instead of verbose if (x == 0) throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException patterns.

data.name: AL0048AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use non-negative guard helper

data.name: AL0048AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'Guard.NotNegative({0})' instead of if/throw pattern

data.name: AL0048AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use Guard.NotNegative() instead of verbose if (x < 0) throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException patterns.

data.name: AL0049AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use positive-guard helper

data.name: AL0049AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'Guard.Positive({0})' instead of if/throw pattern

data.name: AL0049AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use Guard.Positive() instead of verbose if (x <= 0) throw new ArgumentOutOfRangeException patterns.

data.name: AL0050AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use empty-guid guard helper

data.name: AL0050AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'Guard.NotEmpty({0})' instead of if/throw pattern

data.name: AL0050AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use Guard.NotEmpty() instead of verbose if (guid == Guid.Empty) throw new ArgumentException patterns.

data.name: AL0051AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use defined-enum guard helper

data.name: AL0051AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use 'Guard.DefinedEnum({0})' instead of if/throw pattern

data.name: AL0051AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Use Guard.DefinedEnum() instead of verbose if (!Enum.IsDefined) throw new ArgumentException/ArgumentOutOfRangeException patterns.

data.name: AL0052AnalyzerTitle
data.value: [AotSafe] code must not call [AotUnsafe] code

data.name: AL0052AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Method '{0}' is marked [AotSafe] but calls '{1}' which is marked [AotUnsafe]

data.name: AL0052AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Code marked with [AotSafe] guarantees it will work correctly in AOT-compiled applications. Calling methods marked with [AotUnsafe] violates this guarantee because [AotUnsafe] code explicitly declares it requires JIT compilation.

data.name: AL0053AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Unnecessary [AotUnsafe] attribute

data.name: AL0053AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' is marked [AotUnsafe] but contains no AOT-incompatible patterns

data.name: AL0053AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The [AotUnsafe] attribute should only be applied to code that actually uses AOT-incompatible patterns such as reflection, dynamic code generation, or calls to methods with [RequiresDynamicCode]. Consider removing the attribute if the code is AOT-compatible.

data.name: AL0054AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Diagnostic missing from documentation

data.name: AL0054AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Diagnostics defined in Descriptors.cs should be documented in diagnostics.md. Add documentation for this diagnostic rule.

data.name: AL0055AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Diagnostic missing from release notes

data.name: AL0055AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Diagnostics defined in Descriptors.cs should be tracked in AnalyzerReleases.Shipped.md or AnalyzerReleases.Unshipped.md. Add a release note entry for this diagnostic rule.

data.name: AL0056AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Diagnostic documentation mismatch

data.name: AL0056AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Diagnostic title, severity, or category in documentation does not match Descriptors.cs. Keep documentation in sync with the source of truth.

data.name: AL0057AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid async void methods

data.name: AL0057AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Async void method '{0}' should return Task instead. Only event handlers should use async void.

data.name: AL0057AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Async void methods cannot be awaited, exceptions cannot be caught by the caller, and they complicate unit testing. Use async Task instead, unless the method is an event handler.

data.name: AL0058AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid lock on 'this'

data.name: AL0058AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Do not lock on 'this'. External code could also lock on this instance, causing potential deadlocks.

data.name: AL0058AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Locking on 'this' exposes the lock object to external code. Any code with a reference to this instance can lock on it, potentially causing deadlocks. Use a private readonly object field instead.

data.name: AL0059AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid lock on typeof(T)

data.name: AL0059AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Do not lock on 'typeof({0})'. Type objects are globally visible and external code could also lock on them.

data.name: AL0059AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Type objects obtained from typeof() are globally visible singletons. Any code can obtain the same Type object and lock on it, potentially causing deadlocks. Use a private static readonly object field instead.

data.name: AL0060AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid lock on string

data.name: AL0060AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Do not lock on a string. String literals are interned and shared across the entire application, causing potential deadlocks.

data.name: AL0060AnalyzerDescription
data.value: String literals are interned by the CLR, meaning identical strings across different assemblies share the same object reference. Locking on a string creates cross-assembly synchronization that is rarely intended and can cause deadlocks. Use a private readonly object field instead.

data.name: AL0061AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Activity/Span missing semantic convention attributes

data.name: AL0061AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Activity '{0}' should have semantic convention attributes for '{1}' operations

data.name: AL0061AnalyzerDescription
data.value: OpenTelemetry Activities (Spans) should include semantic convention attributes appropriate for their operation type. This enables correlation, filtering, and analysis in observability backends.

data.name: AL0062AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Deprecated semantic convention

data.name: AL0062AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Attribute '{0}' is deprecated since schema v{1}. Use '{2}' instead.

data.name: AL0062AnalyzerDescription
data.value: This attribute name has been deprecated in the OpenTelemetry semantic conventions. Using the newer attribute name ensures compatibility with modern observability tools.

data.name: AL0063AnalyzerTitle
data.value: ActivitySource should be registered with AddSource()

data.name: AL0063AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: ActivitySource '{0}' is not registered with AddSource() in the tracing configuration

data.name: AL0063AnalyzerDescription
data.value: ActivitySources must be registered with AddSource() in the OpenTelemetry tracing configuration to be included in the trace pipeline. Unregistered sources will not emit spans.

data.name: AL0064AnalyzerTitle
data.value: GenAI span missing required attributes

data.name: AL0064AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: GenAI Activity '{0}' is missing required attribute '{1}'

data.name: AL0064AnalyzerDescription
data.value: GenAI spans must include required semantic convention attributes (gen_ai.system, gen_ai.request.model, gen_ai.operation.name) to enable proper observability and correlation of LLM operations.

data.name: AL0065AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use gen_ai.client.token.usage histogram for token metrics

data.name: AL0065AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Token usage should be recorded using 'gen_ai.client.token.usage' histogram instead of '{0}'

data.name: AL0065AnalyzerDescription
data.value: GenAI token usage metrics should use the gen_ai.client.token.usage histogram instrument as defined by the semantic conventions. This ensures compatibility with standard GenAI observability dashboards.

data.name: AL0066AnalyzerTitle
data.value: GenAI operation name should follow semantic conventions

data.name: AL0066AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: GenAI operation name '{0}' should be one of: chat, text_completion, embeddings

data.name: AL0066AnalyzerDescription
data.value: GenAI operation names should follow the semantic conventions: 'chat' for chat completions, 'text_completion' for text completions, and 'embeddings' for embedding generation.

data.name: AL0067AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Meter should be registered with AddMeter()

data.name: AL0067AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Meter '{0}' is not registered with AddMeter() in the metrics configuration

data.name: AL0067AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Meters must be registered with AddMeter() in the OpenTelemetry metrics configuration to be included in the metrics pipeline. Unregistered meters will not export metrics.

data.name: AL0068AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Metric instrument name should follow naming conventions

data.name: AL0068AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Metric name '{0}' should follow the pattern: namespace.component.metric_name.unit

data.name: AL0068AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Metric instrument names should follow OpenTelemetry naming conventions using dot-separated namespaces and snake_case for individual words. Include the unit as a suffix when applicable.

data.name: AL0069AnalyzerTitle
data.value: ServiceDefaults configuration incomplete

data.name: AL0069AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: ServiceDefaults configuration is missing '{0}'. Add {1} for complete observability setup.

data.name: AL0069AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Complete ServiceDefaults configuration should include tracing, metrics, and logging setup. Missing components may result in incomplete observability data.

data.name: AL0070AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Collector endpoint should use OTLP protocol

data.name: AL0070AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Collector endpoint '{0}' should use OTLP protocol (grpc:// or http://...v1/traces)

data.name: AL0070AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Collector endpoints should use the OTLP protocol for standardized telemetry export. OTLP provides better performance and broader compatibility than proprietary protocols.

data.name: AL0071AnalyzerTitle
data.value: [Meter] class must be partial static

data.name: AL0071AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Class '{0}' with [Meter] attribute must be declared as 'partial static' for source generator support

data.name: AL0071AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Classes decorated with [Meter] attribute must be declared as 'partial static' to allow the source generator to implement the metric recording methods. The generator creates static fields for Meter and instrument instances.

data.name: AL0072AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Metric method must be partial

data.name: AL0072AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Method '{0}' with [{1}] attribute must be declared as 'partial' for source generator support

data.name: AL0072AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Methods decorated with [Counter] or [Histogram] attributes must be declared as 'partial' to allow the source generator to implement the metric recording logic. The generator creates the method body that records values to the corresponding instrument.

data.name: AL0073AnalyzerTitle
data.value: [Traced] attribute requires non-empty ActivitySourceName

data.name: AL0073AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: [Traced] attribute on '{0}' has empty or null ActivitySourceName - provide a valid source name

data.name: AL0073AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The [Traced] attribute requires a non-empty ActivitySourceName to identify the source of spans. This name should match a registered ActivitySource in your application's tracing configuration.

data.name: AL0074AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Deprecated GenAI semantic convention

data.name: AL0074AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: GenAI attribute '{0}' is deprecated. Use '{1}' instead.

data.name: AL0074AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Some GenAI semantic convention attributes have been renamed or deprecated. Using the current attribute names ensures compatibility with modern observability tools and dashboards that expect the latest conventions.

data.name: AL0075AnalyzerTitle
data.value: High-cardinality tag on metric

data.name: AL0075AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Tag '{0}' on metric is high-cardinality and may cause memory issues in metrics backends

data.name: AL0075AnalyzerDescription
data.value: High-cardinality tags like user.id, request.id, or session.id create a unique time series for each distinct value. This can overwhelm metrics backends with memory and storage costs. Consider using these values as span attributes instead, or aggregate them into buckets.

data.name: AL0076AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Missing OpenTelemetry configuration

data.name: AL0076AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: AddServiceDefaults() is called but AddOpenTelemetry() is missing. Telemetry will not be exported without OpenTelemetry configuration.

data.name: AL0076AnalyzerDescription
data.value: When using AddServiceDefaults() or similar setup methods, ensure AddOpenTelemetry() is also called to configure telemetry export. Without OpenTelemetry configuration, traces, metrics, and logs will not be exported to observability backends.

data.name: AL0077AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Duplicate instrumentation detected

data.name: AL0077AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Method '{0}' has both [Traced] attribute and manual Activity.StartActivity call. This creates duplicate nested spans.

data.name: AL0077AnalyzerDescription
data.value: When a method is instrumented by both auto-instrumentation (e.g., [Traced] attribute or interceptor) AND manual Activity.StartActivity spans, it creates duplicate nested spans that increase telemetry volume and can cause confusion in trace visualization. Choose one instrumentation approach per method.

data.name: AL0078AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Invalid ActivitySource name

data.name: AL0078AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: ActivitySource name '{0}' does not follow reverse-DNS naming convention. Use format like 'company.product.component'.

data.name: AL0078AnalyzerDescription
data.value: ActivitySource names should follow the reverse-DNS naming convention (e.g., 'company.product.component'). Names must contain at least one dot, use only lowercase letters, digits, dots, and hyphens, and should not contain spaces. This ensures consistent identification across telemetry backends.

data.name: AL0079AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Manual span recommended

data.name: AL0079AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Method '{0}' has [Traced] but contains {1}; manual instrumentation may capture execution better

data.name: AL0079AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Complex async patterns like Task.WhenAll, Parallel.ForEach, multiple awaits, or ConfigureAwait(false) may not be fully captured by auto-instrumentation. Consider using manual Activity.StartActivity() to create explicit child spans for parallel operations, ensuring each branch of execution is properly traced.

data.name: AL0080AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Missing resilience configuration

data.name: AL0080AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: HTTP client '{0}' registered without resilience policies. Consider adding AddStandardResilienceHandler() for retry, timeout, and circuit breaker.

data.name: AL0080AnalyzerDescription
data.value: HTTP clients should be configured with resilience policies to handle transient failures gracefully. Use AddStandardResilienceHandler() from Microsoft.Extensions.Http.Resilience or AddResilienceHandler() with custom Polly policies. This ensures automatic retries, timeouts, and circuit breakers for improved reliability.

data.name: AL0081AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Missing health checks

data.name: AL0081AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Service doesn't expose health check endpoint. Add builder.Services.AddHealthChecks() for container orchestration.

data.name: AL0081AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Health checks are essential for container orchestration platforms (Kubernetes, Docker Swarm) to determine service availability. Add builder.Services.AddHealthChecks() and app.MapHealthChecks() to expose /health endpoints for liveness and readiness probes.

data.name: AL0082AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Consider using configuration for connection string

data.name: AL0082AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Hardcoded connection string detected. Use builder.Configuration.GetConnectionString() instead for environment flexibility.

data.name: AL0082AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Connection strings should be loaded from configuration (appsettings.json, environment variables, or secrets) rather than hardcoded. This enables different connection strings per environment (dev/staging/production) and avoids committing sensitive credentials to source control.

data.name: AL0083AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Insecure endpoint

data.name: AL0083AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: HTTP endpoint '{0}' should use HTTPS for production security. Consider using https:// instead.

data.name: AL0083AnalyzerDescription
data.value: HTTP endpoints expose data in transit to interception and man-in-the-middle attacks. Use HTTPS (TLS) for all production endpoints to ensure data confidentiality and integrity. Localhost and 127.0.0.1 are excluded as they are typically used for local development only.

data.name: AL0084AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Missing service discovery

data.name: AL0084AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Direct URL '{0}' used instead of service discovery. Consider using service name resolution (e.g., 'http+https://servicename') for dynamic endpoint discovery.

data.name: AL0084AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Hardcoded URLs in HttpClient.BaseAddress or service client configuration reduce flexibility and complicate deployment across environments. When using .NET Aspire or service discovery libraries, use service names (e.g., 'http+https://apiservice') that resolve to actual endpoints at runtime. This enables automatic endpoint discovery, load balancing, and environment-specific configuration.

data.name: AL0085AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Invalid attribute value

data.name: AL0085AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Attribute '{0}' has invalid value '{1}'. Expected: {2}

data.name: AL0085AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Attribute values must conform to the expected format defined by OpenTelemetry semantic conventions. For example, http.response.status_code must be an integer, gen_ai.system must be a known provider (openai, anthropic, etc.), and error.type should be an exception type or error code. Invalid values may cause issues with telemetry backends and dashboards.

data.name: AL0086AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Incorrect attribute type

data.name: AL0086AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Attribute '{0}' should be type '{1}' but '{2}' was provided

data.name: AL0086AnalyzerDescription
data.value: OpenTelemetry semantic conventions specify expected types for attributes. For example, gen_ai.usage.input_tokens should be an integer, not a string. Using incorrect types may cause issues with telemetry backends or dashboards that expect specific types for aggregation and visualization.

data.name: AL0087AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Prefer constant attribute

data.name: AL0087AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' instead of string literal "{1}" for attribute name

data.name: AL0087AnalyzerDescription
data.value: OpenTelemetry semantic convention attribute names should use constant fields from GenAiAttributes or SemanticConventions classes instead of string literals. Using constants prevents typos, enables IntelliSense, and ensures consistency with the latest semantic conventions.

data.name: AL0088AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Sensitive data in span attribute

data.name: AL0088AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Attribute '{0}' may contain sensitive data (PII or credentials). Consider redacting or excluding this information.

data.name: AL0088AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Span attributes containing passwords, secrets, tokens, API keys, or PII (SSN, credit card numbers) can leak sensitive information to telemetry backends. Consider redacting sensitive values or excluding these attributes from telemetry. If the data is intentionally included, suppress this warning with a justification comment.

data.name: AL0089AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Missing OTLP configuration

data.name: AL0089AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: OTLP exporter configured without explicit endpoint. Set OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT environment variable or pass endpoint in code.

data.name: AL0089AnalyzerDescription
data.value: When using UseOtlpExporter() or AddOtlpExporter() without an explicit endpoint configuration, telemetry will be sent to the default localhost endpoint (localhost:4317 for gRPC, localhost:4318 for HTTP) or fail to export if no collector is running. Configure OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT environment variable or pass the endpoint explicitly in options to ensure reliable telemetry export.

data.name: AL0090AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Uncompressed OTLP export

data.name: AL0090AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: OTLP exporter using HTTP protocol without compression. Enable gzip compression to reduce bandwidth usage.

data.name: AL0090AnalyzerDescription
data.value: OTLP HTTP protocol does not enable compression by default, unlike gRPC which uses compression automatically. For HTTP exports, especially with large payloads like gen_ai.content attributes, enabling gzip compression significantly reduces bandwidth usage and export latency. Configure ExportProcessorOptions or OtlpExporterOptions to enable compression.

data.name: AL0092AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Consider configuring sampling

data.name: AL0092AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: OpenTelemetry tracing configured without sampling. Consider using ParentBasedSampler or TraceIdRatioBasedSampler to reduce telemetry volume in production.

data.name: AL0092AnalyzerDescription
data.value: High-volume services can generate excessive telemetry data without sampling configuration. AlwaysOnSampler captures every span, which may overwhelm backends and increase costs. Consider using ParentBasedSampler (respects parent decisions) or TraceIdRatioBasedSampler (probabilistic) for production environments. Tail-based sampling at the collector level is also recommended for capturing important traces like errors.

data.name: AL0093AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Missing resource attributes

data.name: AL0093AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: OpenTelemetry resource is missing '{0}'. Add ResourceBuilder.CreateDefault().AddService() to configure service identification.

data.name: AL0093AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Resource attributes 'service.name' and 'service.version' are required for proper service identification in observability backends. These attributes enable grouping traces by service and tracking version-specific behavior. Use ResourceBuilder.CreateDefault().AddService(serviceName, serviceVersion) to set them.

data.name: AL0091AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Batch export disabled

data.name: AL0091AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: SimpleSpanProcessor or SimpleActivityExportProcessor exports spans one at a time. Use BatchSpanProcessor or BatchActivityExportProcessor for better performance.

data.name: AL0091AnalyzerDescription
data.value: SimpleSpanProcessor and SimpleActivityExportProcessor export each span immediately as it completes, which incurs significant overhead. BatchSpanProcessor and BatchActivityExportProcessor collect spans and export them in batches, reducing network overhead and improving application performance. Use batch processors in production environments.

data.name: AL0094AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid 'dynamic' keyword in AOT-published code

data.name: AL0094AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Usage of 'dynamic' is incompatible with Native AOT. The '{0}' operation requires System.Reflection.Emit which is not available at runtime.

data.name: AL0094AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The 'dynamic' keyword requires System.Reflection.Emit at runtime to generate call sites, which is not available in Native AOT. Replace with statically typed alternatives such as interfaces, generics, or pattern matching.

data.name: AL0095AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid Expression.Compile() in AOT context

data.name: AL0095AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' falls back to interpreted mode in Native AOT, which is significantly slower. Consider using source generators or direct code instead.

data.name: AL0095AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Expression.Compile() and Expression.CompileToMethod() rely on System.Reflection.Emit to generate IL at runtime. In Native AOT, these fall back to an interpreted mode that is significantly slower. Consider using source generators, direct code, or compile-time expression evaluation instead.

data.name: AL0096AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Enable EventSourceSupport for AOT with telemetry

data.name: AL0096AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: PublishAot is enabled but EventSourceSupport is not set to true. EventPipe-based telemetry (OpenTelemetry, dotnet-trace, dotnet-counters) requires EventSourceSupport.

data.name: AL0096AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Native AOT trims EventSource/EventPipe infrastructure by default. If the application uses OpenTelemetry, dotnet-trace, dotnet-counters, or other EventPipe-based diagnostics, set EventSourceSupport to true in the project file to preserve this infrastructure.

data.name: AL0104AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Prefer 'await using' for IAsyncDisposable

data.name: AL0104AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' implements IAsyncDisposable. Use 'await using' instead of 'using' to ensure DisposeAsync() is called.

data.name: AL0104AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Types implementing IAsyncDisposable should be disposed with 'await using' in async methods. Using 'using' calls the synchronous Dispose() method instead of DisposeAsync(), which may skip async cleanup such as flushing buffers, closing network connections, or finalizing telemetry export.

data.name: AL0105AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid blocking calls in async methods

data.name: AL0105AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' blocks the calling thread inside an async method. Use 'await' instead to avoid potential deadlocks.

data.name: AL0105AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Calling .Result, .Wait(), or .GetAwaiter().GetResult() on a Task inside an async method blocks the thread pool thread and can cause deadlocks when a SynchronizationContext is present (ASP.NET, WPF). Use 'await' instead to asynchronously wait for the result.

data.name: AL0106AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid Task.Run in ASP.NET Core request handlers

data.name: AL0106AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Task.Run adds unnecessary overhead in ASP.NET Core handlers. Request handling already runs on thread pool threads.

data.name: AL0106AnalyzerDescription
data.value: ASP.NET Core request handling already runs on thread pool threads. Using Task.Run to offload work to another thread pool thread adds scheduling overhead and context switching without benefit. For CPU-bound work that genuinely needs to not block the request, consider using a background service or suppress this diagnostic with a justification.

data.name: AL0101AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Activator.CreateInstance is not AOT-safe

data.name: AL0101AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Activator.CreateInstance<{0}>() uses reflection and is not compatible with NativeAOT. Use explicit construction or factory methods.

data.name: AL0101AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Activator.CreateInstance uses reflection to create instances at runtime. In Native AOT, this requires the target type's constructor to be preserved. Use explicit construction, factory patterns, or annotate with [DynamicallyAccessedMembers(DynamicallyAccessedMemberTypes.PublicParameterlessConstructors)].

data.name: AL0102AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Type.GetType with dynamic name is not AOT-safe

data.name: AL0102AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Type.GetType with {0} prevents the trimmer from statically analyzing which types to preserve. Use typeof() or a string literal.

data.name: AL0102AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Type.GetType(string) with a dynamic type name or case-insensitive search prevents the trimmer from statically analyzing which types to preserve. Use typeof() for compile-time type references. For string literals without case-insensitive search, the trimmer can analyze the call statically.

data.name: AL0107AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Orphaned [TracedTag]

data.name: AL0107AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: [TracedTag] on '{0}' has no effect because neither the method nor its declaring type has [Traced]

data.name: AL0107AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The [TracedTag] attribute is only meaningful when used on parameters of methods that participate in tracing via [Traced]. When neither the method nor its declaring type has [Traced], the tag will be silently ignored and becomes dead metadata.

data.name: AL0108AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Redundant [NoTrace]

data.name: AL0108AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: [NoTrace] on '{0}' has no effect because the declaring type has no class-level [Traced]

data.name: AL0108AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The [NoTrace] attribute is used to opt a method out of class-level [Traced] auto-instrumentation. When the declaring type does not have [Traced], the [NoTrace] attribute has no effect and should be removed to avoid confusion.

data.name: AL0109AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Non-interceptable [Traced]

data.name: AL0109AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: [Traced] on '{0}' will be ignored because abstract, extern, and partial methods cannot be intercepted

data.name: AL0109AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The [Traced] attribute triggers compile-time interception to create spans. Abstract, extern, and partial definition methods cannot be intercepted by the source generator, so [Traced] will be silently ignored.

data.name: AL0110AnalyzerTitle
data.value: [TracedTag] on out/ref parameter

data.name: AL0110AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: [TracedTag] on '{0}' is not supported for out or ref parameters

data.name: AL0110AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The [TracedTag] attribute records parameter values as span attributes at the start of method execution. Parameters with out or ref modifiers are not supported because out parameters have no value at method entry and ref parameters may be mutated during execution.

data.name: AL0111AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid SQL string interpolation in CommandText

data.name: AL0111AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: CommandText uses string interpolation which may be vulnerable to SQL injection; use parameterized queries instead

data.name: AL0111AnalyzerDescription
data.value: String interpolation in SQL CommandText can lead to SQL injection vulnerabilities. Use parameterized queries with placeholders ($1, @param) instead of interpolating values directly.

data.name: AL0112AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid fire-and-forget task discard

data.name: AL0112AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Discarded task from '{0}' will silently swallow exceptions; await the task or add explicit error handling

data.name: AL0112AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Discarding a Task with _ = causes fire-and-forget behavior where exceptions are silently lost. Await the task, store it for later observation, or add a continuation with error handling.

data.name: AL0113AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Missing exception recording on Activity error status

data.name: AL0113AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Activity.SetStatus(Error) without recording the exception; call AddEvent or RecordException to capture exception details

data.name: AL0113AnalyzerDescription
data.value: When setting an Activity status to Error in a catch block, the exception should be recorded as an event on the span for proper observability. Use activity.AddEvent with exception details or a RecordException helper.

data.name: AL0114AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Prefer TryParse over Parse

data.name: AL0114AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}.Parse' throws on invalid input; use '{0}.TryParse' instead

data.name: AL0114AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Parse methods throw FormatException on invalid input. Use TryParse to safely handle invalid input without exceptions, especially when processing user input or external data.

data.name: AL0115AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Empty catch block swallows exceptions

data.name: AL0115AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Catch block is empty; add logging, rethrow, or handle the exception

data.name: AL0115AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Empty catch blocks silently swallow exceptions, making bugs invisible. At minimum, log the exception. If the exception is truly expected, add a comment explaining why it is safe to ignore.

data.name: AL0116AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Exception details leaked in HTTP response

data.name: AL0116AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' exposes exception internals to the client; return a generic error message instead

data.name: AL0116AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Returning exception messages, stack traces, or ToString() output in HTTP responses can expose sensitive implementation details. Return a generic error message and log the exception server-side.

data.name: AL0117AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Unnecessary LINQ materialization

data.name: AL0117AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' after '{1}' forces immediate materialization; consider removing if deferred execution suffices

data.name: AL0117AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Calling ToList() or ToArray() immediately after Where(), Select(), or other LINQ operators forces eager evaluation. If the result is only enumerated once, deferred execution avoids allocating an intermediate collection.

data.name: AL0118AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Read-modify-write without transaction

data.name: AL0118AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Method '{0}' performs both read and write operations without a transaction; wrap in BeginTransaction to ensure consistency

data.name: AL0118AnalyzerDescription
data.value: A method that reads data and then writes based on that data without a transaction is vulnerable to race conditions. Another connection could modify the data between the read and write. Use BeginTransaction/BeginTransactionAsync to ensure isolation.

data.name: AL0119AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid storing ISymbol in source generator models

data.name: AL0119AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Member '{0}' stores ISymbol-derived type '{1}' which breaks incremental generator caching

data.name: AL0119AnalyzerDescription
data.value: ISymbol types do not implement value equality. Storing them in source generator models causes the incremental generator cache to be invalidated on every keystroke, defeating the purpose of incremental generation. Extract the needed data as strings or primitive values instead.

data.name: AL0120AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use IIncrementalGenerator instead of ISourceGenerator

data.name: AL0120AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Type '{0}' implements ISourceGenerator; use IIncrementalGenerator for better IDE performance

data.name: AL0120AnalyzerDescription
data.value: ISourceGenerator (v1 API) runs on every keystroke without caching. IIncrementalGenerator provides an incremental pipeline with automatic caching, significantly improving IDE responsiveness.

data.name: AL0121AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Avoid NormalizeWhitespace in source generators

data.name: AL0121AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: NormalizeWhitespace() is expensive; use raw string literals or manual formatting for source generation

data.name: AL0121AnalyzerDescription
data.value: SyntaxFactory.NormalizeWhitespace() traverses the entire syntax tree to rewrite whitespace, which is expensive in source generators that run frequently. Use raw string literals or manual formatting instead.

data.name: AL0122AnalyzerTitle
data.value: [DuckDbTable] type must be partial

data.name: AL0122AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Type '{0}' has [DuckDbTable] but is not partial; the source generator cannot extend it

data.name: AL0122AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The DuckDbInsertGenerator source generator creates additional methods (AddParameters, MapFromReader, BuildMultiRowInsertSql) on types marked with [DuckDbTable]. The type must be partial so the generator can add these members.

data.name: AL0123AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Conflicting [DuckDbColumn] ordinal values

data.name: AL0123AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Properties '{0}' and '{1}' on type '{2}' both specify Ordinal = {3}

data.name: AL0123AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Multiple properties in a [DuckDbTable] type have the same Ordinal value in their [DuckDbColumn] attribute. Each column must have a unique ordinal to ensure deterministic column ordering in generated SQL.

data.name: AL0124AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Non-interceptable [AgentTraced]

data.name: AL0124AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: [AgentTraced] on '{0}' will be ignored because abstract, extern, and partial methods cannot be intercepted

data.name: AL0124AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The [AgentTraced] attribute triggers compile-time interception to create agent spans. Abstract, extern, and partial definition methods cannot be intercepted by the source generator, so [AgentTraced] will be silently ignored.

data.name: AL0125AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use *Any* string comparison extension

data.name: AL0125AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{0}' to collapse chained equality checks

data.name: AL0125AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Chained string equality comparisons (s == "a" || s == "b") can be collapsed into a single EqualsAnyOrdinal/EqualsAnyIgnoreCase call.

data.name: AL0126AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Forward CancellationToken to invocations that support it

data.name: AL0126AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' has an overload that accepts a CancellationToken

data.name: AL0126AnalyzerDescription
data.value: When a CancellationToken is available in scope and the invoked method has an overload accepting one, forward it to enable cooperative cancellation.

data.name: AL0127AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Outdated MAF ecosystem package version

data.name: AL0127AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Package '{0}' version '{1}' is outdated; minimum required is '{2}'

data.name: AL0127AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Detects outdated Microsoft Agent Framework and related package versions. Update to the minimum required version to ensure compatibility with GA APIs and AIFunctionFactoryOptions.

data.name: AL0128AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Destructive Loom tool must require approval

data.name: AL0128AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Loom tool '{0}' has destructive side effect '{1}' but does not require approval. Add [RequiresApproval] to ensure governance.

data.name: AL0128AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Loom tools with destructive side effects (WritesExternalState, MutatesCode, Deploys, ClosesIssue) must declare [RequiresApproval] to ensure governance gates are in place before execution.

data.name: AL0129AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Loom tool should declare its side effect

data.name: AL0129AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Loom tool '{0}' does not declare its side effect. Add [ToolSideEffect] to make governance explicit.

data.name: AL0129AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Every Loom tool should declare its side effect via [ToolSideEffect] on the method or containing type so that governance policies can be enforced automatically.

data.name: AL0130AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Loom tool should declare required capabilities

data.name: AL0130AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Loom tool '{0}' does not declare any required capabilities. Add [RequiresCapability] to enable fine-grained access control.

data.name: AL0130AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Every Loom tool should declare its required capabilities via [RequiresCapability] on the method or containing type to enable fine-grained access control.

data.name: AL0131AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Direct GenAI SDK call bypasses automatic OTel instrumentation

data.name: AL0131AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Direct {0} SDK call detected. Register the client via AddChatClient(...).AsBuilder().UseFunctionInvocation().UseOpenTelemetry(...).Build() to produce telemetry automatically.

data.name: AL0131AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Calling a GenAI SDK type directly prevents automatic OpenTelemetry span emission. Wrap the provider client through Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient so all LLM calls are traced without changes to call sites.

data.name: AL0132AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Deprecated semantic convention value

data.name: AL0132AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Value '{1}' for attribute '{0}' is deprecated. {2}

data.name: AL0132AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Some semantic convention attribute values have been renamed or removed. Using current values keeps telemetry compatible with modern OpenTelemetry tooling and schema expectations.

data.name: AL0133AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Context-sensitive deprecated semantic convention

data.name: AL0133AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Attribute '{0}' is deprecated. {1}

data.name: AL0133AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Some deprecated semantic convention attributes cannot be migrated with a simple 1:1 rename. This analyzer highlights attributes that require context-sensitive migration or have no direct replacement.

data.name: AL0134AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use OpenTelemetry semantic convention constant

data.name: AL0134AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Hardcoded semantic convention string "{1}". Use the typed constant '{0}' from OpenTelemetry.SemanticConventions instead.

data.name: AL0134AnalyzerDescription
data.value: OpenTelemetry semantic convention attribute names should reference the typed const fields published by the OpenTelemetry.SemanticConventions package (for example HttpAttributes.AttributeHttpRequestMethod) rather than hand-written string literals. Typed constants protect against typos, stay in sync with the schema version that is actually referenced, and enable tooling to diff telemetry surface area across versions.

data.name: AL0135AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Replace legacy SemanticConventions accessor

data.name: AL0135AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' is a legacy OpenTelemetry semantic convention accessor. Use the grouped '{1}' constant instead.

data.name: AL0135AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The aggregated SemanticConventions / TraceSemanticConventions / ResourceSemanticConventions static classes are deprecated in favour of per-domain grouped attribute classes (HttpAttributes, ServerAttributes, DbAttributes, etc.). The new grouping mirrors the upstream OpenTelemetry semantic conventions layout and produces smaller, more navigable diffs on convention upgrades.

data.name: AL0136AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Incubating semantic convention referenced from library

data.name: AL0136AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Instrumentation library references incubating semantic convention '{0}' directly. Copy the definition into a local const to insulate against breaking changes on minor version updates.

data.name: AL0136AnalyzerDescription
data.value: OpenTelemetry SemanticConventions.Incubating members ship without stability guarantees; their name or value can change across minor versions. Instrumentation libraries that embed incubating references force transitive consumers to upgrade in lockstep. The recommended pattern is to copy the definition into the library's own constants file; see https://opentelemetry.io/docs/specs/semconv/non-normative/code-generation/#stability-and-versioning.

data.name: AL0137AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use Guard.* helpers instead of throw helpers

data.name: AL0137AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: Use '{1}' from ANcpLua.Roslyn.Utilities instead of '{0}'

data.name: AL0137AnalyzerDescription
data.value: The BCL throw helpers on ArgumentNullException, ArgumentException, and ArgumentOutOfRangeException — and the Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.If* helpers from Microsoft Agent Framework — only throw; they don't return the validated value, so they can't be used in expressions, member initialisers, or chained validation. The Guard.* helpers from ANcpLua.Roslyn.Utilities return the validated value, share the same [CallerArgumentExpression] parameter-name machinery, and compose. Covers BCL ThrowIfNull / NullOrEmpty / NullOrWhiteSpace and the seven ArgumentOutOfRangeException.ThrowIf* helpers, plus MAF Throw.IfNull / IfNullOrMemberNull / IfNullOrEmpty / IfNullOrWhitespace / IfZero / IfLessThan / IfGreaterThan / IfLessThanOrEqual / IfGreaterThanOrEqual / IfOutOfRange. Cold-path-only Throw.ArgumentNullException / ArgumentOutOfRangeException / ArgumentException / InvalidOperationException are intentionally out of scope — they don't validate, just throw. Banned in qyl-aligned projects via BannedSymbols.txt; this analyzer carries the matching auto-fix.

data.name: AL0138AnalyzerTitle
data.value: Use Math.Round/MathF.Round overload with explicit MidpointRounding

data.name: AL0138AnalyzerMessageFormat
data.value: '{0}' uses banker's rounding by default; pass MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero (or another explicit mode) for predictable behavior

data.name: AL0138AnalyzerDescription
data.value: Math.Round / MathF.Round overloads without a MidpointRounding argument default to ToEven (banker's rounding), which surprises most callers — 0.5 rounds to 0, not 1. Always pass MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero for the natural "round 0.5 up" behavior, or another mode that matches the domain. Banned in qyl-aligned projects via BannedSymbols.txt; this analyzer carries the matching auto-fix that appends MidpointRounding.AwayFromZero to the call.
